title: zoysia pungens var. tenuifolia
intro: Zoysia pungens var. tenuifolia, also known as Japanese wild rye, is a type of grass native to East Asia that has been traditionally used for medicinal purposes. The plant has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which contribute to its medicinal value, and it is also believed to possess antioxidant and antimicrobial properties. Consuming the plant or using its extracts is said to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and even lowering cholesterol levels. In traditional herbal medicine, Zoysia pungens var. tenuifolia can be prepared in various ways, including being made into tea, capsules, or infusions, to treat a range of conditions.
